# Brand Voice Guide Template

## Purpose
This document defines how your brand sounds, feels, and communicates across all social media platforms. It ensures consistency, builds recognition, and creates emotional connections with your audience.

**Instructions:**
1. Define your brand voice attributes with specific examples
2. Adapt tone by platform while maintaining core voice
3. Share this guide with all content creators
4. Review and update quarterly

---

## 1. BRAND VOICE FOUNDATION

### 1.1 Brand Voice Definition
**Brand Voice** is the consistent personality and style of your brand's communication. It reflects who you are and creates recognition across all touchpoints.

**Our Brand Voice in One Sentence:**
_[[Describe your brand voice in a single, memorable sentence]]_

**Example:** *"We sound like a knowledgeable friend who makes complex topics simple and fun."*

### 1.2 Voice vs. Tone
- **Voice** = Who you are (consistent across all platforms)
- **Tone** = How you adapt to situations (varies by context and platform)

### 1.3 Brand Voice Attributes

Choose 3-5 attributes that define your brand voice:

| Attribute | Definition | In Action | Not In Action |
|-----------|------------|-----------|---------------|
| **[[ATTRIBUTE_1]]**<br>*(e.g., Knowledgeable)* | [[Definition]] | [[Example of DO]] | [[Example of DON'T]] |
| **[[ATTRIBUTE_2]]**<br>*(e.g., Approachable)* | [[Definition]] | [[Example of DO]] | [[Example of DON'T]] |
| **[[ATTRIBUTE_3]]**<br>*(e.g., Witty)* | [[Definition]] | [[Example of DO]] | [[Example of DON'T]] |
| **[[ATTRIBUTE_4]]**<br>*(e.g., Bold)* | [[Definition]] | [[Example of DO]] | [[Example of DON'T]] |
| **[[ATTRIBUTE_5]]**<br>*(e.g., Empathetic)* | [[Definition]] | [[Example of DO]] | [[Example of DON'T]] |

---

## 2. PLATFORM-SPECIFIC TONE GUIDELINES

### 2.1 LinkedIn Tone
**Context:** Professional network, B2B focus, career-oriented

**Tone Characteristics:**
- [ ] Professional but not stuffy
- [ ] Insightful and thought-leading
- [ ] Conversational but respectful
- [ ] Data-driven when possible

**Voice Adaptation:**
| Voice Attribute | LinkedIn Expression |
|-----------------|---------------------|
| [[ATTRIBUTE_1]] | [[How it shows up]] |
| [[ATTRIBUTE_2]] | [[How it shows up]] |
| [[ATTRIBUTE_3]] | [[How it shows up]] |

**Do's:**
- [ ] Share industry insights and expertise
- [ ] Use professional but accessible language
- [ ] Include data and statistics
- [ ] Ask thought-provoking questions
- [ ] Engage in meaningful discussions

**Don'ts:**
- [ ] Use excessive slang or casual language
- [ ] Post overly personal content
- [ ] Be overly promotional
- [ ] Use too many emojis (max 1-2 per post)

**Example Post:**
> [[Write an example LinkedIn post in your brand voice]]

---

### 2.2 Instagram Tone
**Context:** Visual platform, lifestyle focus, younger demographic

**Tone Characteristics:**
- [ ] Visual-first storytelling
- [ ] Aspirational yet authentic
- [ ] Emoji-friendly
- [ ] Community-focused

**Voice Adaptation:**
| Voice Attribute | Instagram Expression |
|-----------------|----------------------|
| [[ATTRIBUTE_1]] | [[How it shows up]] |
| [[ATTRIBUTE_2]] | [[How it shows up]] |
| [[ATTRIBUTE_3]] | [[How it shows up]] |

**Do's:**
- [ ] Use storytelling captions
- [ ] Include emojis naturally (3-5 per post)
- [ ] Ask engaging questions
- [ ] Use hashtags strategically
- [ ] Show personality and behind-the-scenes

**Don'ts:**
- [ ] Write walls of text without breaks
- [ ] Be overly salesy
- [ ] Ignore comments and DMs
- [ ] Post low-quality visuals

**Example Post:**
> [[Write an example Instagram caption in your brand voice]]

---

### 2.3 Facebook Tone
**Context:** Community platform, broad demographics, local focus

**Tone Characteristics:**
- [ ] Community-oriented
- [ ] Conversational and friendly
- [ ] Shareable content
- [ ] Customer service mindset

**Voice Adaptation:**
| Voice Attribute | Facebook Expression |
|-----------------|---------------------|
| [[ATTRIBUTE_1]] | [[How it shows up]] |
| [[ATTRIBUTE_2]] | [[How it shows up]] |
| [[ATTRIBUTE_3]] | [[How it shows up]] |

**Do's:**
- [ ] Encourage community discussion
- [ ] Share user-generated content
- [ ] Respond to comments promptly
- [ ] Use Facebook-native features (polls, events)

**Don'ts:**
- [ ] Post content that feels too corporate
- [ ] Ignore negative feedback
- [ ] Over-post (1-2x/day max)

**Example Post:**
> [[Write an example Facebook post in your brand voice]]

---

### 2.4 X/Twitter Tone
**Context:** Real-time, concise, conversation-heavy, news-oriented

**Tone Characteristics:**
- [ ] Concise and punchy
- [ ] Timely and relevant
- [ ] Conversational
- [ ] Witty when appropriate

**Voice Adaptation:**
| Voice Attribute | X/Twitter Expression |
|-----------------|---------------------|
| [[ATTRIBUTE_1]] | [[How it shows up]] |
| [[ATTRIBUTE_2]] | [[How it shows up]] |
| [[ATTRIBUTE_3]] | [[How it shows up]] |

**Do's:**
- [ ] Keep it brief (under 280 characters when possible)
- [ ] Engage in conversations and threads
- [ ] Use trending hashtags appropriately
- [ ] Share timely thoughts and commentary
- [ ] Retweet and reply to others

**Don'ts:**
- [ ] Write long, unbroken paragraphs
- [ ] Be overly formal
- [ ] Ignore replies and mentions
- [ ] Post without context

**Example Tweet:**
> [[Write an example tweet in your brand voice]]

---

### 2.5 TikTok Tone
**Context:** Short-form video, Gen Z focus, entertainment-driven, trends

**Tone Characteristics:**
- [ ] Fun and entertaining
- [ ] Authentic and unpolished
- [ ] Trend-aware
- [ ] Relatable

**Voice Adaptation:**
| Voice Attribute | TikTok Expression |
|-----------------|-------------------|
| [[ATTRIBUTE_1]] | [[How it shows up]] |
| [[ATTRIBUTE_2]] | [[How it shows up]] |
| [[ATTRIBUTE_3]] | [[How it shows up]] |

**Do's:**
- [ ] Participate in trends authentically
- [ ] Show personality and humor
- [ ] Create educational content that's fun
- [ ] Use trending sounds and effects
- [ ] Be authentic, not overly produced

**Don'ts:**
- [ ] Create overly corporate content
- [ ] Ignore trends completely
- [ ] Be too sales-focused
- [ ] Use heavy text overlays

**Example Hook:**
> [[Write an example TikTok hook/script opening in your brand voice]]

## 3. MESSAGING FRAMEWORK

### 3.1 Key Messages
Define 3-5 core messages that should be woven into all content:

| Priority | Key Message | Supporting Points | When to Use |
|----------|-------------|-------------------|-------------|
| 1 | [[MESSAGE_1]] | • [[POINT_1]]<br>• [[POINT_2]]<br>• [[POINT_3]] | [[CONTEXT]] |
| 2 | [[MESSAGE_2]] | • [[POINT_1]]<br>• [[POINT_2]]<br>• [[POINT_3]] | [[CONTEXT]] |
| 3 | [[MESSAGE_3]] | • [[POINT_1]]<br>• [[POINT_2]]<br>• [[POINT_3]] | [[CONTEXT]] |
| 4 | [[MESSAGE_4]] | • [[POINT_1]]<br>• [[POINT_2]]<br>• [[POINT_3]] | [[CONTEXT]] |
| 5 | [[MESSAGE_5]] | • [[POINT_1]]<br>• [[POINT_2]]<br>• [[POINT_3]] | [[CONTEXT]] |

### 3.2 Message Hierarchy
**Primary Message** (always present): [[MESSAGE]]

**Secondary Messages** (rotate based on context):
- [[MESSAGE_1]]
- [[MESSAGE_2]]
- [[MESSAGE_3]]

### 3.3 Value Proposition Statement
_[[Your core value proposition expressed in your brand voice]]_

**Example:** *"We help small businesses grow without the big-business budget."*

## 4. VOCABULARY & LANGUAGE GUIDE

### 4.1 Words We Use
Words and phrases that reflect our brand voice:

| Category | Words/Phrases |
|----------|---------------|
| **Core Vocabulary** | [[WORD_1]], [[WORD_2]], [[WORD_3]], [[WORD_4]] |
| **Action Words** | [[WORD_1]], [[WORD_2]], [[WORD_3]], [[WORD_4]] |
| **Descriptive Words** | [[WORD_1]], [[WORD_2]], [[WORD_3]], [[WORD_4]] |
| **Transition Words** | [[WORD_1]], [[WORD_2]], [[WORD_3]], [[WORD_4]] |

### 4.2 Words We Avoid
Words and phrases that don't fit our brand:

| Word/Phrase | Why We Avoid It | Alternative |
|-------------|-----------------|-------------|
| [[WORD_1]] | [[REASON]] | [[ALTERNATIVE]] |
| [[WORD_2]] | [[REASON]] | [[ALTERNATIVE]] |
| [[WORD_3]] | [[REASON]] | [[ALTERNATIVE]] |
| [[WORD_4]] | [[REASON]] | [[ALTERNATIVE]] |

### 4.3 Industry Jargon Policy
- [ ] **Avoid jargon completely** - explain everything in plain English
- [ ] **Use jargon with explanation** - define terms when first used
- [ ] **Use jargon for specific audiences only** - on LinkedIn for professionals

### 4.4 Grammar & Style Preferences
| Element | Preference | Example |
|---------|------------|---------|
| **Contractions** | [[USE / AVOID]] | [[EXAMPLE]] |
| **Oxford Comma** | [[USE / AVOID]] | [[EXAMPLE]] |
| **Sentence Length** | [[SHORT / MIXED / LONG]] | [[GUIDELINE]] |
| **Emoji Usage** | [[SPARING / MODERATE / LIBERAL]] | [[GUIDELINE]] |
| **Capitalization** | [[TITLE CASE / SENTENCE CASE]] | [[EXAMPLE]] |

### 5.2 Response Guidelines

#### Positive Comments
**Tone:** [[TONE]]

**Template Responses:**
- "[[RESPONSE_1]]"
- "[[RESPONSE_2]]"
- "[[RESPONSE_3]]"

#### Questions
**Tone:** [[TONE]]

**Template Responses:**
- "[[RESPONSE_1]]"
- "[[RESPONSE_2]]"
- "[[RESPONSE_3]]"

#### Complaints/Negative Feedback
**Tone:** [[TONE - usually empathetic, helpful, solution-oriented]]

**Template Responses:**
- "[[RESPONSE_1]]"
- "[[RESPONSE_2]]"
- "[[RESPONSE_3]]"

**Process:**
1. [[STEP_1]]
2. [[STEP_2]]
3. [[STEP_3]]

#### Crisis Situations
**Tone:** [[TONE - usually serious, transparent, accountable]]

_[See Crisis Response Playbook for detailed guidelines]_

## 8. VOICE CHECKLIST

Before publishing any content, verify:

- [ ] Does this sound like our brand?
- [ ] Would our target audience connect with this?
- [ ] Is the tone appropriate for the platform?
- [ ] Does it align with our voice attributes?
- [ ] Are we using our preferred vocabulary?
- [ ] Are we avoiding words from our "avoid" list?
- [ ] Is the message clear and consistent?
- [ ] Would a team member recognize this as "us"?
- [ ] Have we proofread for tone consistency?
- [ ] Does it feel authentic, not forced?
